title: Classic Storage Bed — King
summary: A king-size storage bed built as three separate plywood storage benches — two matching side benches and one wider end bench — with optional drawers in every opening.
description:
The whole bed is really three independent boxes, not one long structure, and that's what makes a king-size storage bed manageable to build and move: each bench is built, finished, and can even be carried into the room on its own before the three are joined together with a few screws. The two side benches use the exact same construction as a queen-size version of this bed; only the end bench's length changes to span the king's extra width.

The drawers are optional and built to the openings you actually end up with, not a fixed size — measure the gap between the mounted slides, subtract the slide clearance (usually about 1" total), and build each drawer box to that number. Getting the slide height mark right (measured up from the bottom edge of the mounting board) is what keeps a pair of opposing slides level with each other; skip it and a drawer front comes out visibly tilted.

tools.note: Trims drawer faces down for a perfect fit.

materials.name: Cabinet-grade plywood, 3/4", 4x8 sheet
materials.note: MDF or AC plywood cost less for a painted finish.

materials.name: Drawer slides, 18"
materials.note: Optional drawers — 6 slides total across the openings.

cutList.note: Measure and adjust to fit — even 1/8" gap on all sides.

steps.title: Cut the parts — and check your sheet count first
steps.body: Every plywood part in this bed is 19-3/4" deep, so break the sheets down by ripping 19-3/4" strips and crosscutting from those. Cut for the two side benches: four sides at 60", twelve dividers at 14-3/4", and eight tops/bottoms at 28-7/8". For the end bench: two sides at 76" (the cut list says one, but a box needs two), three dividers at 14-3/4", and two top/bottom at 36-7/8". From 1/4" plywood cut four side bench backs at 15-1/2" x 60", one end bench back at 15-1/2" x 76", and, if you are building drawers, two bottoms at 18" x 34-3/8". From 1x2 stock: four 60" rails, one 76" rail, twenty-six stiles at 11-1/4", and two side trim at 20-3/4". From 1x4: four back cleats at 60", one 76" trim rail, and two wide side trim at 20-3/4". Buy from this part list, not from the materials quantities — the two 3/4" sheets listed hold roughly 9,200 square inches while the parts above come to about 16,600, and the 1x2 and 1x4 counts fall similarly short. Lay every part out on paper against your real sheets before you buy or cut.

steps.title: Build the two side benches
steps.body: Each side bench is a plywood box 19-3/4" deep and 60" long with six fixed dividers inside it, making it a run of cubbies rather than one open box. Join every joint with 3/4" pocket holes and 1-1/4" pocket hole screws — three per joint is plenty in plywood — and glue as you go. Space the six dividers so the openings between them come out equal, laying out from the centre outward and using a cut scrap as a spacer so every opening matches, because a drawer built for one opening has to fit the next. Check both diagonals on each box before the last screws go in. Then screw a 60" 1x4 cleat along the back of each bench: those are what the mattress slats will rest on, so set them at the same height on both benches, measured the same way on each.

steps.title: Build the end bench
steps.body: The end bench is the same construction as the side benches and the only piece that changes between the queen and king versions — its 76" sides and 36-7/8" top and bottom are what span the king's extra width. Build it the same way: 3/4" pocket holes, 1-1/4" pocket hole screws, three per joint, glue everywhere, with the three 14-3/4" dividers evenly spaced and set with a scrap spacer. Note the cut list gives one 76" side where the box needs two — cut two. Check the diagonals before the glue sets, because this is the longest box in the build and the easiest one to rack.

steps.title: Attach the backs
steps.body: Attach the 1/4" plywood backs to both the side benches and the end bench with 3/4" brad nails and glue.

steps.title: Build and attach the face frames
steps.body: Build each face frame first with 3/4" pocket holes and 1-1/4" pocket hole screws, then nail the completed face frames to the front of each bench with 3/4" brad nails.

steps.title: Build and attach the end bench side trim
steps.body: Build the end bench's side trim face frame first, then attach with 3/4" brad nails to the sides of the end bench.

steps.title: Assemble the three benches
steps.body: Carry the three finished benches into the room separately — that is the whole reason this bed is built as three boxes — and set them in a U with the end bench across the head and a side bench down each side. Screw the abutting corners together so the U cannot spread. Then cut scrap plywood to bridge the cavity between the benches and screw it down onto the mattress-slat cleats on the backs of the two side benches, which is what the mattress will actually rest on. Check the whole assembly is level and that the cleats on all three benches sit at one height before the mattress goes on.

steps.title: Install the drawer slides (optional)
steps.body: Draw a line 1" up from the bottom edge of each drawer-side mounting board. Attach a drawer slide to each board with its front edge flush to the board's front edge and the slide's center holes lined up on your marked line, so every slide is installed level. Remember the opposing slide in each pair installs as a mirror image. Set the mounting boards inside the cubbies, front edges flush with the cabinet fronts, and nail in place with 3/4" brad nails.

steps.title: Build the drawer boxes (optional)
steps.body: Measure the distance between the mounted slide boards, subtract the slide clearance (usually about 1" total), and build each drawer box to that width.

steps.title: Install the drawers and faces (optional)
steps.body:
Install the drawer boxes into the slides. Cut the drawer faces and adjust to fit, sanding down edges or trimming with a table saw as needed for an even gap of about 1/8" on all sides.

Sand every surface and finish.

images.alt: King size storage bed built from three plywood benches
